channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: South Carolina House Passes New Map Aimed at Forcing Out Clyburn
item.description: The changes include a redraw of the congressional district held by James E. Clyburn, the Democratic power broker. The map now goes to the State Senate.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina

item.title: Georgia Supreme Court Justices Fend Off Left-Leaning Challengers
item.description: The Republican-appointed incumbents had faced well-financed opponents who infused issues like abortion rights into the nonpartisan elections.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas
item.media:credit: Kate Brumback/Associated Press
item.media:description: The Nathan Deal Judicial Center in Atlanta, home of the state’s Supreme Court and its Court of Appeals.

item.title: Conservative Legislator Wins Republican Nomination for Oregon Governor
item.description: Christine Drazan’s victory in the Republican primary sets up a rematch with Gov. Tina Kotek, a Democrat, in November.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin
item.media:credit: Jordan Gale for The New York Times
item.media:description: Christine Drazan, a Republican state senator, won the state’s Republican primary for governor Tuesday.

item.title: Idaho’s Republican Governor Wins Primary in Race for a Third Term
item.description: Gov. Brad Little will be heavily favored in a general election contest against an independent former state Supreme Court justice and a Democrat vowing to undo conservative policies.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin
item.media:credit: Rebecca Noble/Associated Press
item.media:description: Brad Little could become the third Idaho governor to serve three consecutive terms.

item.title: Trump Crushes Republican Dissent: 8 Takeaways From Tuesday’s Primaries
item.description: President Trump unseated Representative Thomas Massie, a top Republican critic in Congress, and also got his way in other primary contests.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Michael Swensen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Thomas Massie of Kentucky ran a robust campaign against his Trump-backed challenger but was defeated on Tuesday night.

item.title: Senate Primary in Alabama to Replace Tommy Tuberville Heads to Runoff
item.description: In the race to replace Senator Tommy Tuberville, Representative Barry Moore was leading a multicandidate field that included Jared Hudson, a former Navy SEAL, and Steve Marshall, the state attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Associated Press
item.media:description: From left, Jared Hudson, Rep. Barry Moore, center and Steve Marshall.

item.title: Democratic leaders denounce a party candidate as ‘antisemitic.’
item.description: Democrats accused Republicans of propping up a fringe candidate in Texas, Maureen Galindo, in hopes of pushing her past a more viable Democrat in a House runoff next week.
item.dc:creator: J. David Goodman
item.media:credit: Katina Zentz/San Antonio Express-News, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Maureen Galindo, left, and Johnny Garcia, candidates for House District 35 in Texas, participated in a forum held by the League of Women Voters of the San Antonio Area earlier this month.

item.title: Former Atlanta Mayor Wins Democratic Primary for Georgia Governor
item.description: Keisha Lance Bottoms will be the Democratic standard-bearer, despite worries about her single term as mayor of the state’s biggest city.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas
item.media:credit: Alyssa Pointer/Reuters
item.media:description: Keisha Lance Bottoms greeted volunteers at a campaign phone bank in Atlanta on Tuesday.

item.title: Bob Brooks, Labor Leader Backed by Top Democrats, Wins Pennsylvania House Primary
item.description: Mr. Brooks will face Representative Ryan Mackenzie, a Republican, in the fall. He earned endorsements from across his party, including Senator Bernie Sanders of Vermont and Gov. Josh Shapiro of Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning and Taylor Robinson
item.media:credit: Michelle Gustafson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Bob Brooks arriving at his watch party on election night at a bar in Bethlehem, Pa.

item.title: Mike Collins and Derek Dooley Head to G.O.P. Senate Runoff in Georgia
item.description: Mr. Collins and Mr. Dooley are set to face each other on June 16 as they compete for the chance to challenge Senator Jon Ossoff, a popular Democrat.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Sean Keenan
item.media:credit: Audra Melton for The New York Times; Daniel Varnado/Atlanta Journal-Constitution, via AP
item.media:description: Representative Mike Collins and Derek Dooley.

item.title: A Tuberville-Jones Rematch Is Set for the Alabama Governor Race
item.description: Senator Tommy Tuberville, a Republican, will face the Democrat that he defeated in 2020, Doug Jones, after both easily won their primaries.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Bob Miller for The New York Times

item.title: Republican Primary for Georgia Governor Will Go to a Runoff
item.description: The state’s lieutenant governor, Burt Jones, will face a billionaire health care executive, Rick Jackson, on June 16 to decide the party’s standard-bearer to succeed Gov. Brian Kemp.
item.dc:creator: Rick Rojas
item.category: Georgia Criminal Case Against Trump (2020 Election)
item.media:credit: Arvin Temkar/Atlanta Journal-Constitution, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Rick Jackson, left, and Burt Jones are the remaining Republicans vying to be the party’s candidate for governor of Georgia.

item.title: Dallas Fort Worth Airport Cancels Hundreds of Flights as Storms Hit Texas
item.description: Much of the state is expected to face heavy rain for the rest of the week.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCann and Mark Walker
item.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Voters in Georgia say they’re wary of efforts to unseat Sanford Bishop.
item.description: fut to unseat Black Democrat in Georgia could backfire, voters say.
item.dc:creator: Brendan Farrington
item.media:credit: Pablo Martinez Monsivais/Associated Press
item.media:description: Representative Sanford Bishop at the State of the Union address in February in Washington. Georgia’s Second Congressional District, which is held by Mr. Bishop, is expected to be redrawn to favor Republicans in the 2028 election.

item.title: Alabama’s Republican Senate Primary: Key Regions to Watch
item.description: Barry Moore has led in numerous polls, but a three-way race is likely to advance to a runoff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Lemonides
item.media:credit: Michael Brochstein/Sipa USA, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Representative Barry Moore is a candidate in the Republican Primary for United States Senate in Alabama.

item.title: Thomas Massie Loses Kentucky House Primary to Trump-Backed Ed Gallrein
item.description: Representative Thomas Massie, President Trump’s chief G.O.P. antagonist in the House, lost to Ed Gallrein, Mr. Trump’s handpicked candidate.
item.dc:creator: Robert Draper
item.media:credit: Michael Swensen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rep. Thomas Massie, President Trump’s most prominent Republican antagonist in Congress, was defeated in his primary by a Trump-backed challenger.

item.title: Sen. Gillibrand of New York Clashes With Sean Duffy Over Funding Freeze
item.description: Senator Kirsten Gillibrand, Democrat of New York, criticized the transportation secretary, Sean Duffy, for posting a video that bragged about withholding money from the state.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Kirsten Gillibrand, Democrat of New York, speaking to Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy in a Senate hearing at the Capitol on Tuesday.

item.title: Minnesota Becomes First State to Ban Prediction Markets
item.description: Like at least other 14 states, Minnesota has wrestled with how to regulate the popular prediction market sites. Federal officials sued the state over its new law.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Minnesota State Capitol in St. Paul. Legislators passed a law to make it illegal to host or advertise a prediction market site in Minnesota.

item.title: Early War Goal Was to Install Hard Line Former President as Iran’s Leader
item.description: An Israeli strike designed to free Mahmoud Ahmadinejad from house arrest in Tehran, U.S. officials said, was part of an effort to bring about regime change and put him in power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Former President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad of Iran registering as a candidate in the presidential election in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: What to Know About the Republican Senate Primary in Alabama
item.description: Three leading contenders, including a Trump-backed congressman, are aiming to replace Senator Tommy Tuberville. The winner will be favored in November.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Mike Stewart/Associated Press; Amanda Andrade-Rhoades/Reuters
item.media:description: From left, Attorney General Steve Marshall, Jared Hudson and Representative Barry Moore are facing off in the Senate race in Alabama.

item.title: With Trump’s Settlement, a Possible $100 Million I.R.S. Penalty Melts Away
item.description: The tax service argued that the Trump Organization tried to claim the same losses twice. The president said the audit was a “disgrace.”
item.dc:creator: Russ Buettner
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The audit dated back to a $72.9 million tax refund that Donald J. Trump claimed, and received, starting in about 2010.

item.title: Key Areas to Watch in Georgia’s Republican Senate Primary
item.description: Three candidates are jockeying for position in a race that is likely to result in a runoff.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler
item.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters casting ballots in Atlanta on Tuesday.

item.title: Andy Barr Wins Republican Primary for McConnell’s Seat in Kentucky
item.description: Mr. Barr, who serves in the House, consolidated support among Republicans in part by securing the endorsement of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti
item.media:credit: Jon Cherry/Associated Press
item.media:description: Representative Andy Barr’s win over Daniel Cameron, long seen as a protégé of Senator Mitch McConnell, amounts to a capstone to Mr. McConnell’s decades-long dominance in Kentucky politics.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Mike Collins, Republican Senate Candidate in Georgia
item.description: The congressman is a Trump acolyte who has a history of incendiary social media posts.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Miguel Martinez/Atlanta Journal-Constitution, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Rep. Mike Collins’s hard-line views on immigration are a central part of his campaign message.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Derek Dooley, Republican Senate Candidate in Georgia
item.description: Mr. Dooley is a former football coach running in the Republican primary with the support of Gov. Brian Kemp.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Alyssa Pointer/Associated Press
item.media:description: Derek Dooley, who is supported in the Senate race by Gov. Brian Kemp.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Buddy Carter, Republican Senate Candidate in Georgia
item.description: The congressman is a Trump acolyte who has found some common ground with Democrats on energy issues.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Miguel Martinez/Atlanta Journal-Constitution, via Associated Press
item.media:description: “My hope is that he will endorse me at some point,” Representative Buddy Carter has said of President Trump.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Jon Ossoff, Democratic Senate Candidate in Georgia
item.description: He is the youngest member of the Senate and once volunteered for the civil rights leader John Lewis’s congressional office.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sen. Jon Ossoff, Democrat of Georgia.

item.title: Under a Cloud of Uncertainty, Alabama Voters Head to the Polls
item.description: Voters in Alabama cast their ballots, unsure about whether a recent Supreme Court decision would change their congressional districts going forward.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Bob Miller for The New York Times
item.media:description: People stand outside a polling station at Vaughn Park Church of Christ in Montgomery, Ala., on Tuesday.

item.title: What the Senate Primary Polls Tell Us in Georgia, Alabama and Kentucky
item.description: The Republican primaries in all three states are expected to be competitive, with two of them likely to advance to runoff elections.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler and Alex Lemonides
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters placed their ballots electronically in Independence, Ky.

item.title: Blanche Defends $1.8 Billion Fund as Skepticism Mounts
item.description: The acting attorney general told lawmakers the fund would review claims from an array of people, not just Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Todd Blanche made clear that the fund was created in response to what Mr. Trump and his allies have argued was unjust overreach by the Biden administration.

item.title: Kentucky Primary Election: Key Areas to Watch
item.description: In a contentious House race, Representative Thomas Massie will look for strength in the Cincinnati suburbs.
item.dc:creator: Christine Zhang

item.title: Senate Votes to Take Up Measure to Force Trump to End Iran War
item.description: With four Republican backers, Democrats won a vote to advance a resolution that would force the president to end hostilities or win authorization from Congress.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: The sliver of G.O.P. skepticism to the president’s handling of the Iran conflict widened last week.

item.title: Two friends are split over who should represent their Kentucky district.
item.dc:creator: Ginny Whitehouse
item.media:credit: Ginny Whitehouse for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mark Bruner, left, and the Rev. Randall Burgin.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Thomas Massie, Republican House Candidate in Kentucky
item.description: Mr. Massie lost to a primary challenger backed by President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Jon Cherry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Thomas Massie faces a primary challenger backed by President Trump.

item.title: 5 Things to Know About Ed Gallrein, Republican House Candidate in Kentucky
item.description: Mr. Gallrein ran with President Trump’s support.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Jon Cherry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ed Gallrein has been joined by President Trump on the campaign trail.

item.title: When to Expect Primary Election Results
item.description: Polls begin closing in some states at 6 p.m. Eastern time, but the counting is already underway in some areas.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Robinson
item.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
item.media:description: A polling site on Tuesday in Atlanta. Some of the Tuesday primary states allow minimal mail-in voting; others rely heavily on it.

item.title: Trump’s Stock Trades Raise Eyebrows. The Family Says He Keeps His Hands Off.
item.description: The Trump Organization says the president’s financial investments are handled by outside firms and that it has no control over the timing or selection of transactions.
item.dc:creator: Ben Protess
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: A flurry of trading activity has cast a spotlight on President Trump’s sprawling investment portfolio.

item.title: Thune Casts Doubt on Trump Settlement Fund Plan
item.description: The top Senate Republican cited “blowback” to the idea, while the chairwoman of the Senate Appropriations Committee questioned its legal basis.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator John Thune, Republican of South Dakota and the majority leader, during a news conference at the Capitol on Tuesday.

item.title: What to Know About House Primaries as Massie Faces Trump-Backed Challenger in Kentucky
item.description: In Kentucky, Thomas Massie is fighting off a Trump-backed challenger. Democrats in Pennsylvania have a big decision to make.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kruse
item.media:credit: Michael Swensen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Election Day on Tuesday in the Cold Spring First Baptist Church in Cold Spring, Ky., which is in Thomas Massie’s congressional district.

item.title: Redistricting is making the midterms less competitive.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Wu

item.title: 25 States Sue Over Changes Limiting Federal Loans for Nursing Degrees
item.description: Governors and attorneys general from the states and District of Columbia argue the Education Department’s decision not to label nursing as a professional degree will contribute to worker shortages.
item.dc:creator: Michael C. Bender
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Education Secretary Linda McMahon speaks in the Oval Office of the White House earlier this month.

item.title: Tornado Wipes Out Brand-New Home in Nebraska
item.description: The tornado in Nebraska stayed on the ground for 11 minutes. A new subdivision called Dirt Road was wiped out, while nearby homes went untouched.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
item.media:description: A tornado ripped through a field near St. Libory, Neb., on Sunday.

item.title: Amid Global Turmoil, Trump Shows Off White House Ballroom Construction
item.description: President Trump showed off the construction on his ballroom project, providing a deeper look at the things that matter to him.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
White House East Wing and Ballroom (Washington, DC)
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump during a tour of the East Wing construction project on Tuesday.

item.title: The San Diego Mosque Shootings Were a Crime Made for and by the Internet
item.description: Authorities said the teenagers who killed three men met online, posted their writings there and livestreamed the killings.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango, Chelsia Rose Marcius, Madison Malone Kircher and Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)
item.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
item.media:description: A view of the Islamic Center of San Diego following a deadly mass shooting at the mosque on Monday.

item.title: In Kentucky’s 4th District Primary, Polls Show Tight Race Between Massie and Gallrein
item.description: With support from President Trump, Ed Gallrein is challenging Representative Thomas Massie.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler
item.media:credit: Madeleine Hordinski for The New York Times

item.title: Pentagon Inspector General to Assess U.S. Boat Strikes
item.description: The inspector general’s office said it would evaluate whether U.S. Southern Command followed a six-part process for carrying out airstrikes.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has called those killed in the strikes “narco-terrorists,” but the Trump administration has not provided evidence of drug smuggling.

item.title: Brad Raffensperger Defends Embattled Georgia Election Workers
item.description: Brad Raffensperger, state’s top election official, is hoping to qualify for a runoff in the Republican primary for governor.
item.dc:creator: Johnny Kauffman
item.media:credit: Dustin Chambers for The New York Times
item.media:description: Georgia Secretary of State Brad Raffensperger, a Republican, held a campaign news conference for his bid of Governor in Atlanta.
